title: 1893 Dorsaly Raptors season
content:
Levi Northcott (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as point guard of the Dorsaly Raptors when they won the interregional playoff crown in 1893.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the interregional playoff crown, Levi Northcott remained with the Dorsaly Raptors for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.

Professional career.

Levi Northcott joined the Dorsaly Raptors and became a central figure in the club's rebuild during the mid-1888s. By 1892, local press described him as the franchise's most reliable scorer in close games.

Awards and honors.

Following the interregional playoff crown, Levi Northcott received Finals Most Valuable Player recognition and was named to the All-League first team.

Personal life.

Levi Northcott retired to Lunaris and funded youth leagues through a community foundation established in 1895.

Early life and college career.

Levi Northcott played college basketball in Lunaris before declaring for the professional draft. Scouts noted his court vision and leadership, traits that later defined his role as point guard.

Career statistics.

Across eleven professional seasons, Levi Northcott recorded 14,200 points and 3,100 assists. His efficiency rating peaked during the 1893 playoffs.

Legacy.

Historians of the Dorsaly Raptors cite Levi Northcott's 1893 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Lunaris Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Playing style.

As point guard, Levi Northcott preferred structured half-court sets and emphasized defensive rotations. Teammates credited his communication when the Dorsaly Raptors switched to zone coverage late in quarters.

Championship season.

The 1893 interregional playoff crown campaign culminated in a seven-game series. Levi Northcott averaged a team-high 24 points in the final two games, including the clincher at Lunaris Memorial Arena in Lunaris.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the interregional playoff crown, Levi Northcott remained with the Dorsaly Raptors for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.

Professional career.

Awards and honors.
